summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY: The CompositeInputFormat class is an InputFormat capable of performing joins over a set of data sources sorted and partitioned the same way.

CORE LOGIC: The class uses a parse tree to interpret a given string as a composite expression and proxies IF requests to it. It also adds the default set of identifiers to the parser and informs the parser of user-defined types.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS: The class takes a Configuration object as input and sets the format using the setFormat method. It then creates a CompositeRecordReader for the children of this InputFormat as defined in the init expression.

DEPENDENCIES: The class depends on the Parser class for parsing expressions and the InputFormat, RecordReader, and Configuration classes from the Hadoop MapReduce framework.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code is a parser for join expressions.

CORE LOGIC:
The code uses a shift-reduce parser to parse the join expressions.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes a string expression and an optional comparator as input, and it returns a tree of InputFormats.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the Token, NumToken, NodeToken, StrToken, Lexer, Node, WNode, CNode, and CompositeInputSplit classes. It also depends on the Configuration, JobContext, TaskAttemptContext, InputSplit, RecordReader, and StatusReporter classes.
